subject: quietly leaving the church you grew up in
lyrics:
[Verse]
I fold the hymnal closed and let the cover settle
Slide out the back pew while the offering plate is full
The stained glass throws its colors on the carpet
Like it always did, like nothing here is dull

[Verse]
Mama's voice still carries on the third row
Daddy's name is carved into a brick outside
I learned to count my pennies by the candle
Learned to swallow questions, learned to hide

[Chorus]
So I'm leaving with my shoes still in my hand
No slammed door, no thunder, no demand
Just a girl who loved the singing more than the law
Walking out before the final amen call

[Verse]
I don't hate the steeple or the water in the basin
I just outgrew the answers they kept handing me to hold
There's a God somewhere I haven't met in that building
Maybe out here in the morning, in the cold

[Bridge]
Don't tell them I cried in the parking lot
Don't tell them I still hum the songs at night
Some leaving isn't burning, it's just turning
Toward a quieter kind of light

subject: a city built on a sleeping giant
lyrics:
[Verse]
They poured the concrete over something old and breathing
Laid the streetcar tracks across his collarbone
The mayor cut a ribbon where the heartbeat shows at midnight
And called it progress, called it brick and stone

[Chorus]
Oh, this city's sleeping on a giant's chest
Every tower trembles when he turns to rest
We built our whole lives on a someday-no
Slow blues humming where the deep things go

[Verse]
The river bends around a shoulder we don't talk about
The church bells ring a little flat down on the square
Old men in the diner say they felt him sigh in '49
Shook the coffee cups and rattled every chair

[Verse]
I keep my boots beside the bed and one eye on the ceiling
I married a woman who can sleep through anything
But I lie awake and listen to the foundation creaking
Waiting for the day the giant wants to stand

[Bridge]
Let him sleep, Lord, let him sleep a little longer
We got rent to pay and gardens still to grow
But every city's built on something it can't hold down
And someday, baby, someday he will know

subject: studying alone at two in the morning
lyrics:
[Verse]
The lamp makes a little island on the page
Coffee gone cold in a porcelain cup
The city's gone quiet, the buses have stopped
And it's only the night and me, awake

[Chorus]
Two in the morning, soft as the rain
The words start to swim and they swim back again
Nobody's calling, nobody's near
Just the hum of the fridge and the year

[Verse]
I underline a sentence I won't remember
I draw a small bird in the white of the margin
The pencil keeps moving, the heater keeps sighing
And somewhere a clock lets the minutes go thin

[Verse]
My neighbor's window finally went dark an hour ago
The streetlight paints a yellow square across the floor
I tell myself one chapter, then I'll let the morning take me
Then I read it twice and start the chapter more

[Bridge]
There's a sweetness in the loneliness of learning
A quiet little kingdom none can see
When the world's asleep and the lamp is still burning
The knowledge and the night belong to me

subject: being talked over in every meeting
lyrics:
[Verse]
I raise my hand and the room keeps moving
My voice goes under like a stone in the stream
They finish my sentence with the wrong word
And nod at the echo of somebody's dream

[Chorus]
Ay, but I carry a trumpet inside my chest
A whole brass band they will not let rest
Talk over me, talk over me all you please
The song in my throat brings the giants to their knees

[Verse]
Good morning, good idea, sat dying on my tongue
A man repeats it louder and they call the man a star
I smile like the saint above the doorway
And write down every place that they are wrong

[Verse]
My abuela never waited for permission
She sang across the market till the vendors turned to hear
I inherited her lungs and not her courage
But I'm learning, little by little, how to clear

[Bridge]
So tomorrow when the table starts to drown me
I'll stand and let the mariachi rise
No more swallowing the verses that they owe me
I'll sing it loud enough to fill their eyes

subject: texting someone you shouldn't
lyrics:
[Verse]
The phone glows blue at a quarter past midnight
Your name is a bell I should never have rung
My thumb hovers over a door I should bolt shut
But the message is already halfway sung

[Chorus]
Ay, corazón, you traitor, you thief
Typing the words that will bring me to grief
Delete, delete, but I press send instead
And the trumpets play loud in my reckless head

[Verse]
There's a ring on my hand and a vow in the closet
There's a woman asleep with her back to the wall
And here in the dark I'm a fool with a candle
Writing your name on the edge of it all

[Verse]
Three dots are dancing, you're typing me back now
My whole chest is a plaza on fiesta night
God forgive me, the guitars are all laughing
At a man who knows wrong but can't let go of the light

[Bridge]
Tomorrow I'll swear that I'll throw the thing in the river
Tomorrow I'll kneel and I'll promise to mend
But tonight there's a screen and a name and a fever
And a sin that I'm willing to send

subject: a factory closing down
lyrics:
[Verse]
The whistle blew its last at the end of the shift
The gates exhale a rust-colored sigh
Forty years of iron going quiet at once
And the men file out without meeting an eye

[Chorus]
Dance with me once on the oil-stained floor
Where the great machines will turn no more
Hold me close while the lights go gray
We built this city, now it's taken away

[Verse]
María sweeps the locker room one final time
The lockers gape like mouths with nothing to say
The foreman pockets a single steel bolt
A souvenir of the thing they threw away

[Verse]
The smokestack stood like a saint over the rooftops
Now it's a finger pointing at an empty sky
We learned to love the rhythm of the hammers
And nobody told us the rhythm could die

[Bridge]
So play the bandoneón slow and bitter
Let the bellows weep what the men cannot
We'll waltz through the dust of our own undoing
And leave our shadows where the work forgot

subject: forgiving someone who is already gone
lyrics:
[Verse]
I say your name into an empty kitchen
The morning light is doing all your chores
The kettle clicks, the dust turns in the window
And I'm forgiving you across the floor

[Chorus]
I lay it down, I lay it down at last
The weight I carried longer than the past
There's no one here to take it from my hands
So I give it to the air, and the air understands

[Verse]
The things you broke have softened into stories
The sharp parts dulled like sea glass on the shore
I used to keep your wrong inside a locked room
But I don't need the key for that door anymore

[Bridge]
Wherever you are, or wherever you aren't
I'm unclenching a fist I held for years
Not for you, not for you, but for the quiet
That finally has more room than my tears

[Verse]
The candle burns down past the things I can't tell you
The smoke goes up the way a prayer might go
I forgive you, I forgive you, I forgive you
And the room gets lighter, slow

subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
lyrics:
[Verse]
Open bar, bow tie loose, I don't know your name
DJ spinning something old, the lights are low and flame
You caught me by the cake table, pulled me to the floor
Never met you in my life but I'm not asking for more

[Chorus]
Spin, spin, stranger in the strobe
Two lost guests at the edge of the globe
We don't know the bride, we don't know the groom
But we own every inch of this borrowed room

[Verse]
Gold rings on the table, somebody else's vow
We're just two extra plates and we're living in the now
Your heels in your left hand, my jacket on a chair
Last name a mystery, don't care, don't care

[Verse]
The slow songs come and go, we don't slow down
Confetti in your hair like a falling crown
Midnight, the cake's gone, the uncles are asleep
And you and me are dancing on a promise we won't keep

[Bridge]
When the lights come up we'll vanish like the bass
No number, no goodbye, just a name I'll never trace
But for one borrowed night under somebody's chandelier
A stranger and a stranger made a whole world right here

subject: dancing with a stranger at a wedding
lyrics:
[Verse]
The garden lights are strung between the trees
The band is soft, the wine is almost gone
You offered me your hand without a reason
And I took it, and the evening carried on

[Chorus]
Dance with me, stranger, slow as the tide
I don't know your name and I don't need a guide
The bride throws her flowers, the night opens wide
And we sway like the sea on the warm garden side

[Verse]
Your jacket smells of cedar and of candle
My shoes are in my hand beneath the chair
We move like two leaves that the breeze has gathered
And nobody is watching, nobody to care

[Verse]
You tell me half a story, then you let it
Dissolve like sugar in the evening air
The stars come out above the folding tables
And I rest my cheek against your collar there

[Bridge]
When the music stops we'll bow and we'll be strangers
Go home to lives that never overlap again
But the garden and the saxophone will keep it
This one slow dance, this gentle little when

subject: a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
lyrics:
[Verse]
Forty winters I have climbed these iron stairs
Trimmed the wick and watched the black come down
I write to you each night by the light I keep for sailors
And I drop the pages where the gulls won't drown

[Chorus]
Dear sea, dear cold and hungry bride
I've loved you longer than the men who died
Take my words, take my lamp, take my bones at the end
You're the only wife this rock will ever send

[Verse]
The town below forgot my name a decade back
The supply boat comes when the weather lets it through
But every wave that breaks against the foundation
Is a letter coming back to me from you

[Verse]
I signed my name in salt across the logbook
I dream you in the green beneath the foam
My heart is a buoy that the storms keep ringing
And this tower full of light is the closest thing to home

[Bridge]
When they find me cold beside the burning glass
When the keeper's chair is empty and the door swings wide
Don't bury me in churchyard dirt and granite
Just open up the window, let me ride the tide

subject: a panic attack on the subway
lyrics:
[Verse]
The doors slide shut and the dark comes rushing
The map on the wall starts to blur and bend
My heart's a drummer who forgot the tempo
And the tunnel doesn't seem to have an end

[Chorus]
Breathe, just breathe, count the rivets on the ceiling
Breathe, just breathe, name the colors that you see
The whole car's swaying and nobody's noticing
That the floor is falling out from under me

[Verse]
A man reads his paper like the world's not ending
A kid kicks his sneakers, keeping perfect time
I grip the cold pole till my knuckles whiten
And I beg the next station to arrive

[Verse]
There's an ocean in my ears, there's a fist around my throat
The ad above the seats says I deserve the best
I count four stops, I count my own two hands
I tell my racing chest, please, please rest

[Bridge]
And then the platform, and the doors are open
And the air comes in like mercy, like the sun
I step out shaking onto solid concrete
Still alive, still here, the wave is done

subject: the last day of summer before college
lyrics:
[Verse]
The pool's already drained behind the Hendersons'
The ice cream truck went silent down the street
We lay across the hood till the metal cooled beneath us
And watched the heat lift slowly off the heat

[Chorus]
This is the last one, the last one, the last
The whole gold summer collapsing into past
Tomorrow we're scattered like seeds on the wind
So stay on the rooftop, don't let it begin

[Verse]
Your cassette is warbling something we'll forget
The cul-de-sac is glowing like a coal
We carved our names in the wet cement years ago
And now the sidewalk's holding what we stole

[Verse]
The streetlights buzz awake like they're surprised
The moths are writing circles in the glare
We pretend the dawn is something we can argue with
And talk until there's nothing left to spare

[Bridge]
Don't say the word, don't say goodbye, just let it
Dissolve into the reverb and the haze
I'll keep this one, the last warm night of nothing
Louder than all the colder, faster days

subject: taping up the last box when you move out
lyrics:
[Verse]
The rooms ring hollow now the walls are bare
My footsteps echo where the couch had stood
I press the tape across the final carton
And I thank this house the way I know I should

[Chorus]
One more box, Lord, one more box and I'm gone
Thank You for the table where we leaned and carried on
Thank You for the doorway where the babies learned to walk
I'm taping up the years and I can hear the old house talk

[Verse]
There's a pencil line that marked my children growing
A crayon sun some small hand left to stay
I run my palm along the kitchen counter
And I bless the bread we broke here, day by day

[Verse]
The keys feel heavy in my open hand now
The light comes naked through the curtainless glass
Every empty room is humming hallelujah
For the ordinary glory that came to pass

[Bridge]
So I'll carry it forward, all of it, the laughter
The arguments forgiven, the long Sunday rest
A house is just a box that holds the loving
And I'm taping up the last and the best

subject: a panic attack on the subway
lyrics:
[Verse]
Clack-clack on the rail and my pulse hit double
Sweatin' through my collar in the underground rumble
The lights start strobin' and my knees go to jelly
Got a marching band stompin' right here in my belly

[Chorus]
Whoa, the train keeps rollin' but my brain jumped the track
Gimme one good breath, gimme my body back
The bassline's my heartbeat goin' way too quick
Hold on, hold on, gonna ride through this kick

[Verse]
Groove's got a fever and the fever's got me
The poster says relax but I cannot, can't be
A stranger taps his foot to a song I can't hear
While the walls do the cha-cha and they close in near

[Verse]
Four stops, four stops, I can count to the door
Got my feet on the funk of the rattlin' floor
In through the nose, baby, out through the mouth
Gonna James-Brown my way to the platform south

[Bridge]
Then the doors hit open and the cool air slide
Like a horn section bringin' me back to my stride
I step out shakin' but I'm steady, I'm whole
The groove never broke me, it just rattled my soul

subject: the one that got away
lyrics:
[Verse]
I saw your coat on someone else this morning
Same shade of red, and my whole chest went still
Fifteen years and a couple cities later
And you're living on a ridge of my windowsill

[Chorus]
You're the one, you're the one that got away
The letter I rewrote but never could say
I married kind, I built a quiet life
But your name still hums beneath the everyday strife

[Verse]
We were too young or too scared or too stubborn
We let a small fight grow into a wall
You caught the train and I let the platform empty
And I've replayed the not-running every fall

[Verse]
I hope he's good to you, I hope you're laughing
I hope your kitchen smells of something sweet
I'm not asking for the past to come back open
I just carry you down every memory street

[Bridge]
Some loves are houses and some loves are weather
You were the storm that I never learned to name
And if I had one honest hour to spend you
I'd just say thank you, and I'd say I'm still the same
